response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack/backend software engineer with strong web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the resume shows no exposure to SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD design, or deep-learning workload optimization, which are the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The technical stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, MongoDB, web/GIS work) is misaligned with hardware-software co-design and performance architecture for AI chips.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software engineering experience
Some low-latency systems work at Dunnhumby (high-throughput micro-services)
Brief embedded/C/C++ exposure at National Instruments
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or deep-learning optimization background
No evidence of hardware-software co-design or system architecture at the chip level

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator / GPU / DNN workload optimization background
Long tenure as a software craftsman with some performance-sensitive systems work
